seoTitle: Plumridge Lakes vs Point Grey — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Plumridge Lakes and Point Grey. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Plumridge Lakes vs Point Grey: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Plumridge Lakes and Point Grey.
heading: Plumridge Lakes vs Point Grey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Plumridge Lakes, Western Australia and Point Grey, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Grey has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Plumridge Lakes (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Plumridge Lakes or Point Grey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Plumridge Lakes has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Point Grey scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Plumridge Lakes scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Point Grey scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Plumridge Lakes or Point Grey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Grey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
